title: T-045 — fa_bom_view + bom_export_csv Server Action (NPD-d / N-U3)
prompt:
# T-045 — fa_bom_view + CSV export

PRD: 01-NPD-PRD.md §10.7
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create fa_bom_view per §10.7 + bom_export_csv(productCode) Server Action streaming CSV with same column ordering and badges (RM/PM, d365_status).

## Implementation contract
Migration 0052 emits CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW fa_bom_view per spec, joining fa + LATERAL fa_bom_compute + d365_import_cache. Server Action streams text/csv from view rows. RBAC: dashboard.view to read, bom.export to export.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0052_fa_bom_view.sql [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/actions/bom-export-csv.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/actions/__tests__/bom-export-csv.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0052 ran, when SELECT * FROM fa_bom_view WHERE product_code='FA5101', then rows include component_type, component_code, quantity, process_stage, source, d365_status with COALESCE 'Empty' default
2. Given role npd_manager calls bom_export_csv('FA5101'), when run, then response Content-Type='text/csv' and rows match view ordering
3. Given role viewer (no bom.export), when bom_export_csv called, then AuthError thrown

## Test strategy
- RED: 3 tests for view shape, CSV stream, RBAC
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter web test bom-export-csv'

## Risk red lines
- Do not include built FAs without filter
- Do not skip RBAC for export
- Do not bypass tenant RLS
